assumptions: A supported Ubuntu system with a CUDA-capable NVIDIA GPU is available.
The developer can use sudo and reboot the machine.

developer_goal: Install CUDA on Ubuntu with the Debian installer and verify by building and running the nbody sample.

first_success.outcome: CUDA Toolkit is installed on Ubuntu, environment variables are set, and the nbody sample from cuda-samples runs successfully.
first_success.observable_signal: The nbody sample executable runs from its build location after CUDA install and PATH setup.

route_selection.surface: CUDA Quick Start Guide, Ubuntu Debian Installer through nbody
route_selection.rule: Select the lexicographically shortest complete official route to the same first-success outcome after fixing product intent; if peer routes remain consequential, mark needs-human-judgment.

candidates.name: CUDA Quick Start Guide, Ubuntu Debian Installer through nbody
candidates.reason: Recorded as a reconstructable candidate, but public evidence does not close signup-field opacity and/or peer-route lexicographic selection for a verified count.

candidates.name: Ubuntu Runfile Installer
candidates.reason: Peer Ubuntu installer; Debian/network package path is the lighter download variant called out in the guide.

candidates.name: Windows Visual Studio nbody path
candidates.reason: Different OS surface; Linux Ubuntu is a primary documented Linux target.

candidates.name: Pip wheels runtime-only install
candidates.reason: Runtime-focused and does not include the full developer toolkit verification path.

required_path.action: Obtain and install the prerequisites required by the selected NVIDIA Developer candidate route.

required_fields.label: Install or download choices
required_fields.notes: Exact installer, OS, version, or package selectors are release-dependent or peer-branched.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has a runnable environment for the selected candidate.

required_path.action: Follow the documented commands for the selected NVIDIA Developer first-success candidate through its terminal verification.
required_path.observable_result: The nbody sample executable runs from its build location after CUDA install and PATH setup.

prerequisites.description: {'order': 1, 'type': 'hardware', 'requirement': 'A CUDA-capable NVIDIA GPU on a supported Ubuntu system.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1']}

{'order': 2, 'type': 'software', 'requirement': 'sudo, apt, wget, git or equivalent to obtain cuda-samples, and a CMake-capable build environment for the samples.', 'required': True, 'source_ids': ['S1', 'S2']}

external_gates.description: A supported NVIDIA GPU and Ubuntu platform are required for the guide's verification path.
A reboot is required to load NVIDIA drivers before verification.

unavoidable_waits.description: Package download, image pull, or local service startup time for the selected install route.

platform_outcomes.description: Build and run the nbody sample

excluded.reason: Listed as excluded after success in the source record.

sources.title: CUDA Quick Start Guide
evidence_supported: Primary documented developer route and first-success framing

sources.title: NVIDIA cuda-samples
evidence_supported: Supporting official evidence for NVIDIA cuda-samples

uncertainties.question: Which exact cuda-repo package filename and /usr/local/cuda-* version directory apply for the current toolkit release?
uncertainties.impact: Install and PATH commands use placeholders that must be filled from the current CUDA downloads selector.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Resolve against current official docs and a dated fresh-account or cold walkthrough: The Quick Start Guide uses templated package and version paths that change with toolkit releases.

uncertainties.question: What exact current required signup, identity, legal, and entitlement fields does NVIDIA Developer show for a new developer, and which peer route is the lexicographic shortest path?
uncertainties.impact: Without those fields and a closed route choice, action and field counts cannot be verified for peer comparison.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only walkthrough of the live signup and the selected post-login first-success path, with screenshots of required fields and confirmation that peer routes are longer or gated.
